title: Thecophyllum urbanianum
intro: Thecophyllum urbanianum is a plant species within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as a medicinal plant. The health benefits of Thecophyllum urbanianum include its potential use in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Thecophyllum urbanianum include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Thecophyllum urbanianum is classified within the flowering plant family, Metteniusaceae. This family is a small group of flowering plants native to tropical regions. Thecophyllum urbanianum is a species within this family, known for its unique characteristics. Further classification would place Thecophyllum urbanianum within the order Santalales, which includes various families of flowering plants. This order is a part of the larger clade, angiosperms, which encompasses all flowering plants.
